skill.name: code-review
skill.description: Perform comprehensive code reviews covering security, style, performance, and best practices. Use when reviewing code changes before commit or merge, auditing existing code, or checking for vulnerabilities.
skill.summary: Review code changes for security, quality, performance, tests, and maintainability before merge.
skill.author: ANcpLua

security_audit.summary: Manual review found that the high-risk static findings are false positives from instructional examples, Markdown fences, and security checklist text. The only real operational behavior is guidance to run read-only git diff commands, which creates a low external-command risk but no evidence of malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Read-Only Git Commands Suggested
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill suggests running git diff commands to gather review context. These commands are read-only and use fixed arguments, so the risk is limited to normal repository inspection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command block is clearly present and limited to git diff usage. No shell interpolation, network access, or destructive flags are shown.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Execution Terms Are Review Checklist Text
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The eval and exec strings are listed as red flags for reviewers to find in other code. The skill does not execute these functions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 53 is part of a security checklist under red flags. It is not executable code and has no input source.

low_findings.title: Unsafe innerHTML Is an Educational Bad Example
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The innerHTML assignment is shown as the bad side of an XSS example, followed by a safer textContent example. It is not skill runtime code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding section labels the snippet as bad and contrasts it with a good example. This confirms instructional intent.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The many backtick findings are Markdown code fences, inline severity labels, and example snippets. No Ruby or shell backtick execution exists in the skill content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows Markdown formatting and illustrative code examples. There is no executable Ruby, shell, or script file.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Findings Lack Evidence
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The reported weak cryptography and reconnaissance locations do not contain cryptographic algorithms or system reconnaissance commands. No evidence found of malicious probing or crypto misuse.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are description text, a severity table, SQL examples, and error handling examples. None contains MD5, SHA1, host discovery, process listing, or similar behavior.

content.value_statement: Code changes can hide security flaws, regressions, and maintainability problems. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through a structured review before commit or merge.
actual_capabilities: Checks code changes for security issues such as injection, weak authorization, and sensitive data exposure.
Reviews style, naming, formatting, file organization, and import consistency.
Looks for performance problems such as N+1 queries, blocking operations, and inefficient algorithms.
Assesses error handling, logging, test coverage, duplication, and function responsibility.
Produces a severity-based review summary with critical, high, medium, low, and informational sections.
limitations: The skill is a review guide and does not run automated scanners by itself.
Findings depend on the code context and files made available to the assistant.
It may miss issues that require runtime behavior, production logs, or threat modeling.
It does not replace human approval for high-risk security or compliance changes.

use_cases.title: Pre-Merge Pull Request Review
use_cases.description: Review a branch before merge and receive prioritized findings for bugs, security risks, and tests.

use_cases.title: Security-Focused Change Audit
use_cases.description: Check sensitive changes for injection risks, missing authorization, weak validation, and exposed secrets.

use_cases.title: Maintainability Review for Legacy Code
use_cases.description: Audit existing code for oversized functions, duplication, weak error handling, and unclear structure.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Current Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the code-review skill to review my current changes. Focus on security, bugs, tests, and maintainability. Give findings by severity.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Specific File
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the code-review skill on this file or diff. Identify concrete issues with line references and explain the expected fix.

prompt_templates.title: Security and Performance Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the code-review skill to audit this change for injection risks, authorization gaps, sensitive data exposure, N+1 queries, and blocking operations.

prompt_templates.title: Release Gate Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the code-review skill as a release gate. Review the full diff, separate blocking issues from follow-ups, and call out missing tests.

output_examples.input: Review a pull request that changes authentication middleware.
output: High: Permission checks are skipped for one route group.
Medium: Tests cover success cases but not rejected requests.
Low: Error messages can be more consistent with existing middleware.

output_examples.input: Review a database query change for performance and security.
output: Critical: User input is concatenated into a SQL query.
Medium: The loop performs one query per user and can create N+1 behavior.
Info: The new helper name is clear and matches project style.

output_examples.input: Review a refactor before committing it.
output: High: One error path now returns success after a failed parse.
Medium: The refactor removed coverage for invalid input.
Low: A repeated constant should be extracted for readability.

best_practices: Provide the full diff, related files, and expected behavior before asking for review.
Ask for line-specific findings and severity labels so fixes can be prioritized.
Run tests or linters after applying review feedback to confirm behavior.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for approval-only reviews when you need defect discovery.
Do not review isolated snippets when the issue depends on authorization, configuration, or callers.
Do not treat AI findings as final proof without checking the affected code and tests.

faq.question: Does this skill run security scanners?
faq.answer: No. It provides a structured manual review workflow for the assistant to apply to code and diffs.

faq.question: Can it review pull requests?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the diff or let the assistant inspect the changed files in the repository.

faq.question: What severities does it use?
faq.answer: It organizes findings as critical, high, medium, low, and informational.

faq.question: Can it find security vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: It can identify common vulnerability patterns when the relevant code is available for review.

faq.question: Does it replace human code review?
faq.answer: No. It supports reviewers by surfacing issues and questions before human approval.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
